highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.197: Claude Sonnet 5 Now Default with 1M-Token Context
highlights.summary: Claude Code v2.1.197 introduces Claude Sonnet 5 as the new default model, featuring a native 1 million-token context window — enough to load entire large codebases at once. Promotional pricing of $2/$10 per million tokens is available through August 31, making it a great time to upgrade. Run `npm update -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code` to get the latest version.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.196: Organization Default Models & Readable Session Names
highlights.summary: Admins can now set a default model for their entire organization via the org console, which appears as 'Org default' or 'Role default' in the `/model` command for team members. Sessions also now get human-readable names automatically, making it much easier to find and resume past conversations. These are welcome quality-of-life improvements for teams using Claude Code at scale.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.191: `/rewind` Command to Undo `/clear`
highlights.summary: The new `/rewind` command lets you resume a conversation from before you accidentally ran `/clear`, saving you from losing valuable context mid-session. This release also fixes scroll position jumping when reading earlier output during streaming responses, and resolves a bug where stopped background agents would unexpectedly restart. These fixes make long coding sessions significantly smoother.

highlights.title: Claude Code Is Steganographically Marking Requests
highlights.summary: A researcher discovered that Claude Code is embedding hidden steganographic markers in its requests, raising questions about transparency and user privacy. This is generating significant discussion on Hacker News about what data is being embedded, why, and whether users should be concerned. Developers using Claude Code in sensitive or enterprise contexts may want to read the full post before making decisions about their workflows.

highlights.title: Show HN: Smart Model Router for Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor
highlights.summary: Workweave has released an open-source model router that plugs into coding agents like Claude Code and automatically routes each request to the most suitable model, potentially reducing costs and improving response quality. It runs locally and integrates without changing your existing workflow. This is a compelling tool for developers looking to optimize multi-model setups without manual switching.

highlights.title: From Zero to Claude Code in 19 Minutes (No Code)
highlights.summary: This beginner-friendly YouTube tutorial walks complete newcomers through setting up and using Claude Code without any prior coding experience, all in under 20 minutes. It's a great starting point if you've just heard about Claude Code and want to get productive quickly without feeling overwhelmed. Watch it alongside the official docs for the fastest onboarding experience.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This focused YouTube tutorial is designed for developers who are past the basics and want to level up their Claude Code skills efficiently in a single sitting. It covers practical workflows and techniques that go beyond the default setup to help you get more done with the tool. Ideal for anyone who has already installed Claude Code but feels like they're not using it to its full potential.

highlights.title: MiMoCode: Xiaomi's Open-Source AI Coding Assistant Hits 11,000+ GitHub Stars in 19 Days
highlights.summary: Xiaomi's MiMo team has open-sourced MiMoCode, an AI coding assistant featuring a persistent memory system so the AI retains context across sessions, three agent modes, parallel sub-agents, and automated goal evaluation. It can be installed with a single command and is currently free to use. For developers exploring alternatives or complements to Claude Code, this rising project is worth keeping an eye on.

tips_of_the_day: Now that Claude Sonnet 5 supports a 1M-token context window, try feeding it your entire project directory at once using `claude --add-dir .` — you'll get more coherent multi-file refactors without needing to manually manage what context is loaded.

If you're in a team setting, ask your org admin to configure a default model in the Anthropic org console so everyone on the project uses the same model automatically — this prevents inconsistent outputs from teammates using different model versions.

Use the new `/rewind` command as a safety net: if you run `/clear` and immediately regret it, `/rewind` can restore your conversation state. Make it a habit to reach for `/rewind` before starting a brand-new session from scratch.
